purpose: Schedule workspace-owned workflows without changing their contracts or authority boundaries.
invariants: Every automation has exactly one workspaceId.
Every trigger invokes a registered workflowId or actionId through the execution runtime.
Founder approval is never inferred from a schedule or condition.
Evidence, verification, tool permissions, and approval gates remain binding.
Cross-workspace execution and combined mutation contexts are prohibited.
Consequential runs require idempotency keys and immutable audit references.

approvalPolicy.creation: Founder approval required when the automation can lead to consequential, financial, destructive, security-sensitive, or external actions.
approvalPolicy.execution: Any approval gate registered on the target workflow remains mandatory at run time.
approvalPolicy.modification: Material changes to target, cadence, authority, delivery, or consequential scope require Founder approval.
